// <auto-generated/>
//! The `Ordering` bounded context.

use crate::koine_runtime::Decimal;

/// The errors a domain operation in this context can fail with.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum DomainError {
    /// An invariant of `type_name` was violated: `rule`.
    InvariantViolation { type_name: &'static str, rule: &'static str },
    /// Two quantities of different units were combined.
    UnitMismatch { type_name: &'static str },
}

impl std::fmt::Display for DomainError {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
        match self {
            DomainError::InvariantViolation { type_name, rule } => write!(f, "{type_name}: {rule}"),
            DomainError::UnitMismatch { type_name } => write!(f, "{type_name}: units do not match"),
        }
    }
}

impl std::error::Error for DomainError {}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub enum OrderStatus {
    Draft,
    Placed,
    Shipped,
    Cancelled,
}

impl OrderStatus {
    /// Looks up a variant by its declared name (a non-throwing `TryFromName`).
    pub fn from_name(name: &str) -> Option<Self> {
        match name {
            "Draft" => Some(OrderStatus::Draft),
            "Placed" => Some(OrderStatus::Placed),
            "Shipped" => Some(OrderStatus::Shipped),
            "Cancelled" => Some(OrderStatus::Cancelled),
            _ => None,
        }
    }

    /// Looks up a variant by its ordinal value (a non-throwing `TryFromValue`).
    pub fn from_value(value: i64) -> Option<Self> {
        match value {
            0 => Some(OrderStatus::Draft),
            1 => Some(OrderStatus::Placed),
            2 => Some(OrderStatus::Shipped),
            3 => Some(OrderStatus::Cancelled),
            _ => None,
        }
    }

    /// Exhaustively maps the variant to a value — one arm per member, no catch-all.
    pub fn match_<R>(
        &self,
        draft: impl FnOnce() -> R,
        placed: impl FnOnce() -> R,
        shipped: impl FnOnce() -> R,
        cancelled: impl FnOnce() -> R,
    ) -> R {
        match self {
            OrderStatus::Draft => draft(),
            OrderStatus::Placed => placed(),
            OrderStatus::Shipped => shipped(),
            OrderStatus::Cancelled => cancelled(),
        }
    }

    /// Exhaustively dispatches a side effect — one arm per member, no catch-all.
    pub fn switch(
        &self,
        draft: impl FnOnce(),
        placed: impl FnOnce(),
        shipped: impl FnOnce(),
        cancelled: impl FnOnce(),
    ) {
        match self {
            OrderStatus::Draft => draft(),
            OrderStatus::Placed => placed(),
            OrderStatus::Shipped => shipped(),
            OrderStatus::Cancelled => cancelled(),
        }
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub struct OrderLine {
    product: ProductId,
    quantity: i64,
    unit_price: Decimal,
}

impl OrderLine {
    /// Creates a validated `OrderLine`, running its invariants.
    pub fn new(product: ProductId, quantity: i64, unit_price: Decimal) -> Result<Self, DomainError> {
        Ok(Self {
            product,
            quantity,
            unit_price,
        })
    }

    pub fn product(&self) -> &ProductId { &self.product }

    pub fn quantity(&self) -> i64 { self.quantity }

    pub fn unit_price(&self) -> Decimal { self.unit_price }

    pub fn subtotal(&self) -> Decimal {
        self.unit_price * Decimal::from(self.quantity)
    }
}


/// A branded identity value.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub struct OrderId(String);

impl OrderId {
    pub fn new(value: impl Into<String>) -> Self { OrderId(value.into()) }
    pub fn value(&self) -> &str { &self.0 }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct Order {
    id: OrderId,
    lines: Vec<OrderLine>,
    status: OrderStatus,
}

impl PartialEq for Order {
    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool { self.id == other.id }
}
impl Eq for Order {}
impl std::hash::Hash for Order {
    fn hash<H: std::hash::Hasher>(&self, state: &mut H) { self.id.hash(state); }
}

impl Order {
    /// Creates a validated `Order`, running its invariants.
    pub fn new(id: OrderId, lines: Vec<OrderLine>, status: Option<OrderStatus>) -> Result<Self, DomainError> {
        let status = status.unwrap_or(OrderStatus::Draft);
        Ok(Self {
            id,
            lines,
            status,
        })
    }

    pub fn id(&self) -> &OrderId { &self.id }

    pub fn lines(&self) -> &Vec<OrderLine> { &self.lines }

    pub fn status(&self) -> OrderStatus { self.status }
}

/// Persistence-ignorant repository contract for the `Order` aggregate root.
pub trait OrderRepository {
    /// Loads the aggregate by its identity, if present.
    fn get(&self, id: &OrderId) -> Option<Order>;

    /// Persists a new aggregate.
    fn save(&mut self, aggregate: Order);

    /// Persists changes to an existing aggregate.
    fn update(&mut self, aggregate: Order);

    /// Removes the aggregate with the given identity.
    fn remove(&mut self, id: &OrderId);
}

/// A branded identity value.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub struct ProductId(String);

impl ProductId {
    pub fn new(value: impl Into<String>) -> Self { ProductId(value.into()) }
    pub fn value(&self) -> &str { &self.0 }
}
